triggering_commit: dependabot: Update pyright requirement from <=1.1.410 to <=1.1.411 in /src/bindings/python
root_cause_summary: benchmark_app Python sample crashes with Windows STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION (exit code 0xC0000005) when running with -api async -no_warmup on CPU. The app completes all 11 steps and outputs results but crashes on exit during teardown. The C++ variant of the same test passes, implicating the Python binding cleanup/destructor path. The triggering commit (pyright linting update) is unrelated.
notable_findings: benchmark_app async-CPU-C++ PASSED; only async-CPU-Python FAILED
The benchmark app completed all 11 steps and output valid results before crashing
Exit code 3221225477 = 0xC0000005 = STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION (Windows memory access violation)
The triggering commit only changes pyright linting requirement - unrelated to the crash
recommended_actions: Investigate recent changes to the Python benchmark_app sample or Python bindings async/infer-request teardown path on Windows
Search for recently merged PRs that touched samples/python/benchmark_app.py or Python bindings async inference lifecycle
Run benchmark_app Python async mode with no_warmup under a Windows debugger (WinDbg/Dr. Watson) to capture the crash callstack
Check if the crash is reproducible on Linux to narrow platform scope
